---
title: "UO Acute Care Simulation Lab"
---

# Acute Care Simulation Lab

Welcome to the University of Oregon Acute Care Simulation Lab.

Select a simulation below to begin.

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.chart-card}
## 🧠 Simulation 1

### Initial Patient Contact

Acute ischemic stroke with altered mental status.

Students will complete:

- Patient Summary
- Chart Review
- Vital Signs
- Bedside Contact
- Clinical Impression & Recommendations

[Begin Simulation](simulations/sim1/index.qmd)

:::

::: {.chart-card}
## 🧠 Simulation 2

### Cognitive Screening

Right MCA ischemic stroke with left neglect, fluctuating orientation, and memory concerns.

Students will complete:

- Patient Summary
- Chart Review
- Interdisciplinary Notes
- Screening Readiness
- Functional Implications

[Begin Simulation](simulations/sim2/index.qmd)

:::

::: {.chart-card}
## 💬 Simulation 3

### Aphasia Assessment

Left hemisphere stroke with expressive and receptive aphasia following re-hospitalization after a fall.

Students will complete:

- Patient Summary
- Chart Review
- Interdisciplinary Notes
- Communication Considerations
- Functional Implications

[Begin Simulation](simulations/sim3/index.qmd)

:::

::: {.chart-card}
## 📱 Simulation 4

### AAC Evaluation

Patients in acute care may experience communication barriers related to motor impairment, sensory loss, language differences, pain, or medical status.

Students will complete four website-based AAC consultation cases involving:

- Quadriplegia and absent verbal communication
- Visual impairment and pain communication
- Spanish-language access
- Hearing impairment

Students will identify communication barriers, evaluate access abilities, select appropriate AAC supports, coordinate care, and document recommendations.

[Begin Simulation 4](simulations/sim4/index.qmd)

:::

::: {.chart-card}
## 🗣 Simulation 5

### Speaking Valve Assessment

**Coming Soon**

:::

::: {.chart-card}
## 🥣 Simulation 6

### Dysphagia Evaluation

**Coming Soon**

:::

:::
